# Literature database & pillar × theme map {#sec-appendix-database}

This appendix folds the navigable **literature database** into the book. Every row is a paper in the read corpus — the **138** entries the review was built from — each with a clickable DOI, its pillar and theme, and the claim-IDs (`CL-Pn-NN`) it serves. A pillar × theme coverage matrix and a short *how this review was built* reading map follow.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**How to read the table.** Rows are grouped by **primary pillar** (P1–P4; P0 foundational claims appear in the *Claim-IDs served* column, not as a group). *Citekey* is the stable `@citekey` used throughout the book and bibliography; *Reference* is the short author–year–title–venue form; *DOI* links to `https://doi.org/…`; *Claim-IDs served* lists the catalogued claims the paper supports or qualifies; *Tier* (A/B/C) records the reading-priority tier. All 138 papers are owned and read (each has a critique note).
:::

## P1 — The frequentist RVE lineage  ·  47 papers {#sec-appendix-p1}

| Citekey | Reference | DOI | Theme | Claim-IDs served | Tier |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `abadie2023` | Abadie et al. (2022). When should you adjust standard errors for clustering?. *The Quarterly Journal of Economics*. | [10.1093/qje/qjac038](https://doi.org/10.1093/qje/qjac038) | T1;T9 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-02 | B |
| `ahn2012` | Ahn et al. (2012). A review of meta-analyses in education: Methodological strengths and weaknesses. *Review of Educational Research*. | [10.3102/0034654312458162](https://doi.org/10.3102/0034654312458162) | T2 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-04 | C |
| `bell2002` | Bell & McCaffrey (2002). Bias Reduction in Standard Errors for Linear Regression with Multi-Stage Samples. *Survey Methodology*. | no DOI — Survey Methodology (Statistics Canada) assigns no DOI to its pre-2010 issues; 2002 vol. 28(2) article has no publisher DOI | T8 | CL-P1-04, CL-P1-05, CL-P1-07 | A |
| `binder1983` | Binder (1983). On the Variances of Asymptotically Normal Estimators from Complex Surveys. *International Statistical Review*. | [10.2307/1402588](https://doi.org/10.2307/1402588) | T8 | CL-P1-02, CL-P1-03, CL-P3-04, CL-P3-05 | B |
| `cameron2015` | Cameron & Miller (2015). A Practitioner's Guide to Cluster-Robust Inference. *Journal of Human Resources*. | [10.3368/jhr.50.2.317](https://doi.org/10.3368/jhr.50.2.317) | T1 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-02, CL-P1-03, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-05, CL-P1-07 | B |
| `chen2025` | Chen & Pustejovsky (2025). Adapting methods for correcting selective reporting bias in meta-analysis of dependent effect sizes. *Psychological Methods*. | [10.1037/met0000773](https://doi.org/10.1037/met0000773) | T1;T2 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-02, CL-P1-03, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-06, CL-P2-05 | B |
| `clintonlisell2021` | Clinton-Lisell (2021). Stop multitasking and just read: meta-analyses of multitasking's effects on reading performance and reading time. *Journal of Research in Reading*. | [10.1111/1467-9817.12372](https://doi.org/10.1111/1467-9817.12372) | T2 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-04 | C |
| `dewit2021` | De Wit et al. (2021). Procedural learning in individuals with amnestic mild cognitive impairment and alzheimer’s dementia: a systematic review and meta-analysis. *Neuropsychology Review*. | [10.1007/s11065-020-09449-1](https://doi.org/10.1007/s11065-020-09449-1) | T2 | CL-P1-01 | C |
| `efron1975` | Efron & Morris (1975). Data Analysis Using Stein's Estimator and its Generalizations. *Journal of the American Statistical Association*. | [10.1080/01621459.1975.10479864](https://doi.org/10.1080/01621459.1975.10479864) | T2 | CL-P1-01 | C |
| `emslander2022` | Emslander & Scherer (2022). The relation between executive functions and math intelligence in preschool children: A systematic review and meta-analysis. *Psychological Bulletin*. | [10.1037/bul0000369](https://doi.org/10.1037/bul0000369) | T2 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-04 | C |
| `fernandezcastilla2026` | Fernández-Castilla et al. (2026). Meta-regression with categorical moderators and dependent effect sizes: A simulation study. *Research Synthesis Methods*. | [10.1017/rsm.2026.10092](https://doi.org/10.1017/rsm.2026.10092) | T1;T2 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-02, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-05, CL-P1-06, CL-P1-07, CL-P1-08 | B |
| `furenes2021` | Furenes et al. (2021). A comparison of children’s reading on paper versus screen: A meta-analysis. *Review of Educational Research*. | [10.3102/0034654321998074](https://doi.org/10.3102/0034654321998074) | T2 | CL-P1-01 | C |
| `gaskell2023` | Gaskell et al. (2023). The effectiveness of psychological interventions delivered in routine practice: Systematic review and meta-analysis. *Administration and Policy in Mental Health and Mental Health Services Research*. | [10.1007/s10488-022-01225-y](https://doi.org/10.1007/s10488-022-01225-y) | T2 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-04 | C |
| `gnambs2023` | Gnambs & Schroeders (2023). Accuracy and precision of fixed and random effects in meta-analyses of randomized control trials for continuous outcomes. *Research Synthesis Methods*. | [10.1002/jrsm.1673](https://doi.org/10.1002/jrsm.1673) | T1;T11 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-02, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-05, CL-P1-07, CL-P1-08 | B |
| `hartung2001` | Hartung & Knapp (2001). On tests of the overall treatment effect in meta-analysis with normally distributed responses. *Statistics in Medicine*. | [10.1002/sim.791](https://doi.org/10.1002/sim.791) | T2 | CL-P1-02, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-07 | B |
| `hedges2010` | Hedges et al. (2010). Robust Variance Estimation in Meta-Regression with Dependent Effect Size Estimates. *Research Synthesis Methods*. | [10.1002/jrsm.5](https://doi.org/10.1002/jrsm.5) | T1 | CL-P0-01, CL-P0-02, CL-P1-01, CL-P1-02, CL-P1-06 | A |
| `imbens2016` | Imbens & Kolesar (2016). Robust Standard Errors in Small Samples: Some Practical Advice. *Review of Economics and Statistics*. | [10.1162/REST_a_00552](https://doi.org/10.1162/REST_a_00552) | T8 | CL-P1-02, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-05 | A |
| `irsova2025` | Irsova et al. (2025). Spurious precision in meta-analysis of observational research. *Nature Communications*. | [10.1038/s41467-025-63261-0](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-025-63261-0) | T5 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-02, CL-P1-04 | B |
| `joshi2022` | Joshi et al. (2022). Cluster Wild Bootstrapping to Handle Dependent Effect Sizes in Meta-Analysis with a Small Number of Studies. *Research Synthesis Methods*. | [10.1002/jrsm.1554](https://doi.org/10.1002/jrsm.1554) | T2 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-02, CL-P1-03, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-05, CL-P1-06, CL-P1-07, CL-P1-08 | B |
| `jung2020` | Jung et al. (2020). Prosocial modeling: A meta-analytic review and synthesis. *Psychological Bulletin*. | [10.1037/bul0000235](https://doi.org/10.1037/bul0000235) | T2 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-04 | C |
| `koenka2021` | Koenka et al. (2021). A meta-analysis on the impact of grades and comments on academic motivation and achievement: a case for written feedback. *Educational Psychology*. | [10.1080/01443410.2019.1659939](https://doi.org/10.1080/01443410.2019.1659939) | T2 | CL-P1-01 | C |
| `mackinnon2023a` | MacKinnon et al. (2023). Fast and reliable jackknife and bootstrap methods for cluster-robust inference. *Journal of Applied Econometrics*. | [10.1002/jae.2969](https://doi.org/10.1002/jae.2969) | T1 | CL-P1-02, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-05 | B |
| `mackinnon2023b` | MacKinnon et al. (2023). Cluster-Robust Inference: A Guide to Empirical Practice. *Journal of Econometrics*. | [10.1016/j.jeconom.2022.04.001](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2022.04.001) | T1 | CL-P1-02, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-05, CL-P1-08 | B |
| `mackinnon2025` | MacKinnon et al. (2025). Cluster-robust jackknife and bootstrap inference for logistic regression models. *Econometric Reviews*. | [10.1080/07474938.2025.2515161](https://doi.org/10.1080/07474938.2025.2515161) | T1 | CL-P1-02, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-05 | B |
| `nakagawa2012` | Nakagawa & Santos (2012). Methodological issues and advances in biological meta-analysis. *Evolutionary Ecology*. | [10.1007/s10682-012-9555-5](https://doi.org/10.1007/s10682-012-9555-5) | T2 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-06 | C |
| `page2015` | Page et al. (2015). Methods to select results to include in meta-analyses deserve more consideration in systematic reviews. *Journal of Clinical Epidemiology*. | [10.1016/j.jclinepi.2015.02.009](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jclinepi.2015.02.009) | T2 | CL-P1-01 | C |
| `pfeffermann2025` | Pfeffermann et al. (2025). Statistical Inference Under Nonignorable Sampling and Nonresponse–-An Empirical Likelihood Approach. *Journal of Survey Statistics and Methodology*. | [10.1093/jssam/smaf015](https://doi.org/10.1093/jssam/smaf015) | T2 | CL-P1-02, CL-P3-03 | C |
| `pustejovsky2018` | Pustejovsky & Tipton (2018). Small-sample methods for cluster-robust variance estimation and hypothesis testing in fixed effects models. *Journal of Business & Economic Statistics*. | [10.1080/07350015.2016.1247004](https://doi.org/10.1080/07350015.2016.1247004) | T1 | CL-P1-02, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-05, CL-P1-07, CL-P1-08 | B |
| `pustejovsky2022` | Pustejovsky & Tipton (2022). Meta-Analysis with Robust Variance Estimation: Expanding the Range of Working Models. *Prevention Science*. | [10.1007/s11121-021-01246-3](https://doi.org/10.1007/s11121-021-01246-3) | T1 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-02, CL-P1-03, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-06, CL-P1-08, CL-P2-01, CL-P2-05 | A |
| `pustejovsky2023` | Pustejovsky & Tipton (2023). Corrigendum: Small sample methods for cluster-robust variance estimation and hypothesis testing in fixed effects models. *Journal of Business & Economic Statistics*. | [10.1080/07350015.2023.2174123](https://doi.org/10.1080/07350015.2023.2174123) | T1 | CL-P1-04, CL-P1-07 | B |
| `pustejovsky2024` | Pustejovsky & Chen (2024). Equivalencies Between Ad Hoc Strategies and Multivariate Models for Meta-Analysis of Dependent Effect Sizes. *Journal of Educational and Behavioral Statistics*. | [10.3102/10769986241232524](https://doi.org/10.3102/10769986241232524) | T2 | CL-P0-02, CL-P1-01, CL-P1-02, CL-P1-05, CL-P1-06 | A |
| `pustejovsky2025` | Pustejovsky et al. (2025). A Preliminary Data Analysis Workflow for Meta-Analysis of Dependent Effect Sizes. *Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society A*. | [10.31222/osf.io/vfsqx_v2](https://doi.org/10.31222/osf.io/vfsqx_v2) | T1 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-02, CL-P1-03, CL-P1-06 | B |
| `schauer2022` | Schauer et al. (2022). On the bias of complete- and shifting-case meta-regressions with missing covariates. *Research Synthesis Methods*. | [10.1002/jrsm.1558](https://doi.org/10.1002/jrsm.1558) | T2 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-05 | C |
| `sera2019` | Sera et al. (2019). An extended mixed-effects framework for meta-analysis. *Statistics in Medicine*. | [10.1002/sim.8362](https://doi.org/10.1002/sim.8362) | T2 | CL-P1-02, CL-P1-06, CL-P2-05 | B |
| `tannersmith2014` | Tanner-Smith & Tipton (2014). Robust variance estimation with dependent effect sizes: practical considerations including a software tutorial in stata and spss. *Research Synthesis Methods*. | [10.1002/jrsm.1091](https://doi.org/10.1002/jrsm.1091) | T2 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-02, CL-P1-03, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-06, CL-P1-07, CL-P1-08 | B |
| `tannersmith2016` | Tanner-Smith et al. (2016). Handling Complex Meta-Analytic Data Structures Using Robust Variance Estimates: A Tutorial in R. *Journal of Developmental and Life-Course Criminology*. | [10.1007/s40865-016-0026-5](https://doi.org/10.1007/s40865-016-0026-5) | T2 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-02, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-06, CL-P1-07, CL-P1-08 | B |
| `tipton2015a` | Tipton (2015). Small Sample Adjustments for Robust Variance Estimation with Meta-Regression. *Psychological Methods*. | [10.1037/met0000011](https://doi.org/10.1037/met0000011) | T1 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-02, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-05, CL-P1-07 | A |
| `tipton2015b` | Tipton & Pustejovsky (2015). Small-Sample Adjustments for Tests of Moderators and Model Fit Using Robust Variance Estimation in Meta-Regression. *Journal of Educational and Behavioral Statistics*. | [10.3102/1076998615606099](https://doi.org/10.3102/1076998615606099) | T1 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-07 | A |
| `tipton2019` | Tipton et al. (2019). Current practices in meta-regression in psychology, education, and medicine. *Research Synthesis Methods*. | [10.1002/jrsm.1339](https://doi.org/10.1002/jrsm.1339) | T2 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-08 | C |
| `vanhouwelingen2002` | van Houwelingen et al. (2002). Advanced methods in meta-analysis: multivariate approach and meta-regression. *Statistics in Medicine*. | [10.1002/sim.1040](https://doi.org/10.1002/sim.1040) | T2 | CL-P1-06, CL-P2-01, CL-P2-05 | C |
| `vembye2024` | Vembye et al. (2024). Conducting power analysis for meta-analysis with dependent effect sizes: Common guidelines and an introduction to the POMADE R package. *Research Synthesis Methods*. | [10.1002/jrsm.1752](https://doi.org/10.1002/jrsm.1752) | T1 | CL-P1-02, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-07 | B |
| `viechtbauer2010` | Viechtbauer (2010). Conducting meta-analyses in R with the metafor package. *Journal of Statistical Software*. | [10.18637/jss.v036.i03](https://doi.org/10.18637/jss.v036.i03) | T2 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-08 | C |
| `wang2021` | Wang (2021). The Pseudo Maximum Likelihood Estimator for Quantiles of Survey Variables. *Journal of Survey Statistics and Methodology*. | [10.1093/jssam/smz053](https://doi.org/10.1093/jssam/smz053) | T2 | CL-P1-02, CL-P3-03 | C |
| `welz2023` | Welz et al. (2023). Cluster-robust estimators for multivariate mixed-effects meta-regression. *Computational Statistics & Data Analysis*. | [10.1016/j.csda.2022.107631](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.csda.2022.107631) | T1;T11 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-02, CL-P1-03, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-05, CL-P1-06, CL-P1-07, CL-P1-08 | B |
| `williams2025a` | Williams et al. (2025). Modelling Approaches for Meta-Analyses with Dependent Effect Sizes in Ecology and Evolution: A Simulation Study. *Methods in Ecology and Evolution*. | [10.1111/2041-210X.70156](https://doi.org/10.1111/2041-210X.70156) | T1 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-05, CL-P1-06 | B |
| `yang2024` | Yang et al. (2024). Robust point and variance estimation for meta-analyses with selective reporting and dependent effect sizes. *Methods in Ecology and Evolution*. | [10.1111/2041-210x.14377](https://doi.org/10.1111/2041-210x.14377) | T2 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-02, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-05 | B |
| `zejnullahi2024` | Zejnullahi & Hedges (2024). Robust Variance Estimation in Small Meta-Analysis with the Standardized Mean Difference. *Research Synthesis Methods*. | [10.1002/jrsm.1668](https://doi.org/10.1002/jrsm.1668) | T1 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-07 | B |

## P2 — Bayesian meta-analysis & the missing analogue  ·  36 papers {#sec-appendix-p2}

| Citekey | Reference | DOI | Theme | Claim-IDs served | Tier |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `ades2024` | Ades et al. (2024). Twenty years of network meta-analysis: Continuing controversies and recent developments. *Research Synthesis Methods*. | [10.1002/jrsm.1700](https://doi.org/10.1002/jrsm.1700) | T4 | CL-P2-01, CL-P2-03, CL-P2-05, CL-P2-06 | B |
| `bartos2026` | Bartoš et al. (2026). Robust Bayesian multilevel meta-analysis: Adjusting for publication bias in the presence of dependent effect sizes. *Behavior Research Methods*. | [10.3758/s13428-026-03023-y](https://doi.org/10.3758/s13428-026-03023-y) | T3;T11 | CL-P2-01, CL-P2-03, CL-P2-05, CL-P2-06 | B |
| `becker1992` | Becker (1992). Using results from replicated studies to estimate linear models. *Journal of Educational Statistics*. | [10.3102/10769986017004341](https://doi.org/10.3102/10769986017004341) | T5 | CL-P2-05 | B |
| `berkey1998` | Berkey et al. (1998). Meta-analysis of multiple outcomes by regression with random effects. *Statistics in Medicine*. | [10.1002/(sici)1097-0258(19981130)17:22<2537::aid-sim953>3.0.co;2-c](https://doi.org/10.1002/(sici)1097-0258(19981130)17:22<2537::aid-sim953>3.0.co;2-c) | T5 | CL-P1-06, CL-P2-05 | B |
| `bilici2025` | Bilici et al. (2025). Six ways to handle dependent effect sizes in meta-analytic structural equation modeling: Is there a gold standard?. *Research Synthesis Methods*. | [10.1017/rsm.2024.10](https://doi.org/10.1017/rsm.2024.10) | T5;T11 | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-02, CL-P2-01, CL-P2-04, CL-P2-05, CL-P2-06 | B |
| `blowes2026` | Blowes (2026). Location-scale models and cross validation to advance quantitative evidence synthesis. *Ecology*. | [10.1002/ecy.70303](https://doi.org/10.1002/ecy.70303) | T5 | CL-P2-01, CL-P2-05 | B |
| `chen2015` | Chen et al. (2015). An alternative pseudolikelihood method for multivariate random-effects meta-analysis. *Statistics in Medicine*. | [10.1002/sim.6350](https://doi.org/10.1002/sim.6350) | T4 | CL-P2-03, CL-P2-05, CL-P3-04, CL-P3-05 | B |
| `cheung2008` | Cheung (2008). A model for integrating fixed-, random-, and mixed-effects meta-analyses into structural equation modeling. *Psychological Methods*. | [10.1037/a0013163](https://doi.org/10.1037/a0013163) | T5 | CL-P2-04 | B |
| `cheung2013` | Cheung (2013). Multivariate meta-analysis as structural equation models. *Structural Equation Modeling: A Multidisciplinary Journal*. | [10.1080/10705511.2013.797827](https://doi.org/10.1080/10705511.2013.797827) | T5 | CL-P2-04, CL-P2-05 | A |
| `dai2023` | Dai et al. (2023). Quantile regression in random effects meta-analysis model. *Statistical Methods & Applications*. | [10.1007/s10260-022-00660-3](https://doi.org/10.1007/s10260-022-00660-3) | T5;T11 | CL-P2-01, CL-P2-03 | B |
| `du2023` | Du et al. (2023). Bootstrap-based between-study heterogeneity tests in meta-analysis. *Multivariate Behavioral Research*. | [10.1080/00273171.2021.1997701](https://doi.org/10.1080/00273171.2021.1997701) | T5 | CL-P2-03, CL-P2-05 | B |
| `gelman2006` | Gelman (2006). Prior Distributions for Variance Parameters in Hierarchical Models (Comment on Article by Browne and Draper). *Bayesian Analysis*. | [10.1214/06-BA117A](https://doi.org/10.1214/06-BA117A) | T3 | CL-P2-01, CL-P2-05, CL-P3-01 | B |
| `gelman2008` | Gelman et al. (2008). A Weakly Informative Default Prior Distribution for Logistic and Other Regression Models. *The Annals of Applied Statistics*. | [10.1214/08-AOAS191](https://doi.org/10.1214/08-AOAS191) | T3 | CL-P2-01, CL-P2-03 | B |
| `guolo2023` | Guolo (2023). Approximate likelihood and pseudo-likelihood inference in meta-analysis of diagnostic accuracy studies accounting for disease prevalence and study design. *Statistics in Medicine*. | [10.1002/sim.9878](https://doi.org/10.1002/sim.9878) | T4 | CL-P2-05, CL-P2-06, CL-P3-04, CL-P3-05 | B |
| `hong2018` | Hong et al. (2018). An improved method for bivariate meta-analysis when within-study correlations are unknown. *Research Synthesis Methods*. | [10.1002/jrsm.1274](https://doi.org/10.1002/jrsm.1274) | T5 | CL-P1-02, CL-P1-03, CL-P1-04, CL-P2-03, CL-P2-05 | B |
| `hong2020` | Hong et al. (2020). The galaxy plot: A new visualization tool for bivariate meta-analysis studies. *American Journal of Epidemiology*. | [10.1093/aje/kwz286](https://doi.org/10.1093/aje/kwz286) | T5 | CL-P2-01, CL-P2-05 | B |
| `hu2025` | Hu et al. (2025). Multivariate meta-analysis with a robustified diagonal likelihood function. *Journal of Applied Statistics*. | [10.1080/02664763.2025.2487912](https://doi.org/10.1080/02664763.2025.2487912) | T5 | CL-P2-03, CL-P2-05 | B |
| `jackson2011` | Jackson et al. (2011). Multivariate meta-analysis: Potential and promise. *Statistics in Medicine*. | [10.1002/sim.4172](https://doi.org/10.1002/sim.4172) | T5 | CL-P2-01, CL-P2-05 | A |
| `jackson2013a` | Jackson et al. (2013). A matrix‐based method of moments for fitting the multivariate random effects model for meta‐analysis and meta‐regression. *Biometrical Journal*. | [10.1002/bimj.201200152](https://doi.org/10.1002/bimj.201200152) | T5 | CL-P2-01, CL-P2-05 | B |
| `jackson2013b` | Jackson (2013). Confidence intervals for the between-study variance in random effects meta-analysis using generalised cochran heterogeneity statistics. *Research Synthesis Methods*. | [10.1002/jrsm.1081](https://doi.org/10.1002/jrsm.1081) | T5 | CL-P2-05 | B |
| `jackson2024` | Jackson et al. (2024). Multistep estimators of the between-study covariance matrix under the multivariate random-effects model for meta-analysis. *Statistics in Medicine*. | [10.1002/sim.9985](https://doi.org/10.1002/sim.9985) | T5 | CL-P2-01, CL-P2-05 | B |
| `jansen2026` | Jansen & Nestler (2026). Multivariate location-scale models for meta-analysis. *Multivariate Behavioral Research*. | [10.1080/00273171.2026.2643868](https://doi.org/10.1080/00273171.2026.2643868) | T5;T11 | CL-P2-01, CL-P2-03, CL-P2-05 | B |
| `kalaian1996` | Kalaian & Raudenbush (1996). A multivariate mixed linear model for meta-analysis. *Psychological Methods*. | [10.1037/1082-989x.1.3.227](https://doi.org/10.1037/1082-989x.1.3.227) | T5 | CL-P2-01, CL-P2-03, CL-P2-05 | B |
| `liu2025` | Liu et al. (2025). Network meta-analysis made simple: A composite likelihood approach. *Research Synthesis Methods*. | [10.1017/rsm.2024.12](https://doi.org/10.1017/rsm.2024.12) | T4 | CL-P1-04, CL-P2-01, CL-P2-03, CL-P2-05, CL-P2-06 | B |
| `mathur2025` | Mathur (2025). Meta-analysis with jeffreys priors: Empirical frequentist properties. *Research Synthesis Methods*. | [10.1017/rsm.2024.2](https://doi.org/10.1017/rsm.2024.2) | T3 | CL-P2-01, CL-P2-03, CL-P3-01 | B |
| `raudenbush1988` | Raudenbush et al. (1988). Modeling multivariate effect sizes. *Psychological Bulletin*. | [10.1037/0033-2909.103.1.111](https://doi.org/10.1037/0033-2909.103.1.111) | T5 | CL-P1-02, CL-P2-05 | B |
| `rosenthal1986` | Rosenthal & Rubin (1986). Meta-analytic procedures for combining studies with multiple effect sizes. *Psychological Bulletin*. | [10.1037/0033-2909.99.3.400](https://doi.org/10.1037/0033-2909.99.3.400) | T5 | CL-P2-05 | C |
| `si2015` | Si et al. (2015). Bayesian Nonparametric Weighted Sampling Inference. *Bayesian Analysis*. | [10.1214/14-BA924](https://doi.org/10.1214/14-BA924) | T3 | CL-P2-01, CL-P3-03 | C |
| `varin2008` | Varin (2008). On composite marginal likelihoods. *AStA Advances in Statistical Analysis*. | [10.1007/s10182-008-0060-7](https://doi.org/10.1007/s10182-008-0060-7) | T4 | CL-P3-04, CL-P3-05 | B |
| `varin2011` | Varin et al. (2011). An overview of composite likelihood methods. *Statistica Sinica*. | no DOI — Statistica Sinica 2011 article; the journal assigns no DOI to this volume (no publisher DOI exists) | T4 | CL-P3-04, CL-P3-05 | B |
| `viechtbauer2022` | Viechtbauer & López-López (2022). Location-scale models for meta-analysis. *Research Synthesis Methods*. | [10.1002/jrsm.1562](https://doi.org/10.1002/jrsm.1562) | T5 | CL-P2-01 | B |
| `wang2025` | Wang et al. (2025). Exploiting multivariate network meta-analysis: A calibrated Bayesian composite likelihood inference. *Bayesian Analysis*. | [10.1214/25-ba1511](https://doi.org/10.1214/25-ba1511) | T4 | CL-P2-06, CL-P3-09 | A |
| `wei2013` | Wei & Higgins (2013). Estimating within-study covariances in multivariate meta-analysis with multiple outcomes. *Statistics in Medicine*. | [10.1002/sim.5679](https://doi.org/10.1002/sim.5679) | T5 | CL-P1-02, CL-P2-03, CL-P2-05 | B |
| `white2012` | White et al. (2012). Consistency and inconsistency in network meta-analysis: model estimation using multivariate meta-regression. *Research Synthesis Methods*. | [10.1002/jrsm.1045](https://doi.org/10.1002/jrsm.1045) | T4 | CL-P1-06, CL-P2-03, CL-P2-05, CL-P2-06 | B |
| `williams2018` | Williams et al. (2018). Bayesian Meta-Analysis with Weakly Informative Prior Distributions. | [10.31234/osf.io/7tbrm](https://doi.org/10.31234/osf.io/7tbrm) | T3 | CL-P2-01, CL-P2-03, CL-P3-01 | A |
| `xu2026` | Xu et al. (2026). Identifying conditions favouring multiplicative heterogeneity models in network meta-analysis. *arXiv*. | [10.48550/arXiv.2601.11735](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2601.11735) | T4;T5 | CL-P2-01, CL-P2-05 | B |

## P3 — The calibrated-Bayes lens  ·  32 papers {#sec-appendix-p3}

| Citekey | Reference | DOI | Theme | Claim-IDs served | Tier |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `adekpedjou2023` | Adekpedjou & Bindele (2023). Rank-Based Inference for Survey Sampling Data. *Journal of Survey Statistics and Methodology*. | [10.1093/jssam/smab019](https://doi.org/10.1093/jssam/smab019) | T7 | CL-P3-03, CL-P3-04 | C |
| `bissiri2016` | Bissiri et al. (2016). A general framework for updating belief distributions. *Journal of the Royal Statistical Society Series B: Statistical Methodology*. | [10.1111/rssb.12158](https://doi.org/10.1111/rssb.12158) | T6 | CL-P3-01, CL-P3-09 | A |
| `cai2013` | Cai (2013). Investigation of Ways to Handle Sampling Weights for Multilevel Model Analyses. *Sociological Methodology*. | [10.1177/0081175012460221](https://doi.org/10.1177/0081175012460221) | T7 | CL-P3-03, CL-P3-04 | C |
| `chen2019` | Chen & Zhao (2019). Quantile Regression Analysis of Survey Data Under Informative Sampling. *Journal of Survey Statistics and Methodology*. | [10.1093/jssam/smy018](https://doi.org/10.1093/jssam/smy018) | T7 | CL-P3-03, CL-P3-07 | C |
| `frazier2024` | Frazier et al. (2026). Calibrated generalized Bayesian inference. *arXiv*. | [10.48550/arXiv.2311.15485](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2311.15485) | T7 | CL-P3-01, CL-P3-09 | A |
| `hornby2023` | Hornby et al. (2023). csSampling: An R Package for Bayesian Models for Complex Survey Data. | [10.48550/arXiv.2308.06845](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2308.06845) | T7 | CL-P3-03, CL-P3-07, CL-P4-04 | C |
| `lambardidisanminiato2021` | Miniato & Sartori (2021). Adjusted composite likelihood for robust Bayesian meta-analysis. *arXiv*. | [10.48550/arXiv.2104.01920](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2104.01920) | T8 | CL-P2-06, CL-P3-09 | A |
| `leonnovelo2023` | Leon-Novelo & Savitsky (2023). Fully Bayesian Estimation Under Dependent and Informative Cluster Sampling. *Journal of Survey Statistics and Methodology*. | [10.1093/jssam/smab037](https://doi.org/10.1093/jssam/smab037) | T7 | CL-P3-03 | B |
| `li2024` | Li & Rice (2024). A Bayesian “sandwich” for variance estimation. *Statistical Science*. | [10.1214/24-STS935](https://doi.org/10.1214/24-STS935) | T6 | CL-P3-04, CL-P3-05 | B |
| `little2004` | Little (2004). To Model or Not To Model? Competing Modes of Inference for Finite Population Sampling. *Journal of the American Statistical Association*. | [10.1198/016214504000000467](https://doi.org/10.1198/016214504000000467) | T6 | CL-P3-01, CL-P3-02 | B |
| `little2012` | Little (2012). Calibrated Bayes, an Alternative Inferential Paradigm for Official Statistics. *Journal of Official Statistics*. | no DOI — Journal of Official Statistics 2012 vol. 28(3) article; the journal did not register DOIs for its pre-2014 issues, so no publisher/Crossref DOI exists (verified absent via Crossref title + ISSN 0282-423X queries) | T6 | CL-P0-01, CL-P0-02, CL-P3-01, CL-P3-02 | A |
| `lyddon2019` | Lyddon et al. (2019). General Bayesian updating and the loss-likelihood bootstrap. *Biometrika*. | [10.1093/biomet/asz006](https://doi.org/10.1093/biomet/asz006) | T6 | CL-P3-05, CL-P3-09 | A |
| `muller2013` | Müller (2013). Risk of Bayesian inference in misspecified models, and the sandwich covariance matrix. *Econometrica*. | [10.3982/ECTA9097](https://doi.org/10.3982/ECTA9097) | T6 | CL-P3-04, CL-P3-09 | A |
| `nascimento2024` | Nascimento & Gon\ccalves (2024). Bayesian Quantile Regression Models for Complex Survey Data Under Informative Sampling. *Journal of Survey Statistics and Methodology*. | [10.1093/jssam/smae015](https://doi.org/10.1093/jssam/smae015) | T7 | CL-P3-03, CL-P3-04 | C |
| `nascimento2026` | Nascimento & Gon\ccalves (2026). A Bayesian Approach to Multiple-Output Quantile Regression Analysis Under Informative Sampling. *Journal of Survey Statistics and Methodology*. | [10.1093/jssam/smaf040](https://doi.org/10.1093/jssam/smaf040) | T7 | CL-P3-03, CL-P3-07 | C |
| `pfeffermann1993` | Pfeffermann (1993). The Role of Sampling Weights When Modeling Survey Data. *International Statistical Review*. | [10.2307/1403631](https://doi.org/10.2307/1403631) | T8 | CL-P3-04, CL-P3-05 | B |
| `pfeffermann1998` | Pfeffermann et al. (1998). Parametric Distributions of Complex Survey Data Under Informative Probability Sampling. *Statistica Sinica*. | no DOI — Statistica Sinica 1998 article predates the journal's DOI registration; no DOI assigned by the publisher | T7 | CL-P3-03, CL-P3-04 | C |
| `rabehesketh2006` | Rabe-Hesketh & Skrondal (2006). Multilevel Modelling of Complex Survey Data. *Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series A (Statistics in Society)*. | [10.1111/j.1467-985X.2006.00426.x](https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1467-985X.2006.00426.x) | T7 | CL-P3-03, CL-P3-07 | C |
| `rubin1984` | Rubin (1984). Bayesianly Justifiable and Relevant Frequency Calculations for the Applied Statistician. *The Annals of Statistics*. | [10.1214/aos/1176346785](https://doi.org/10.1214/aos/1176346785) | T6 | CL-P0-01, CL-P0-02, CL-P3-01, CL-P3-02 | A |
| `savitsky2016` | Savitsky & Toth (2016). Bayesian Estimation Under Informative Sampling. *Electronic Journal of Statistics*. | [10.1214/16-EJS1153](https://doi.org/10.1214/16-EJS1153) | T7 | CL-P3-03 | A |
| `savitsky2022` | Savitsky & Williams (2022). Pseudo Bayesian Mixed Models under Informative Sampling. *Journal of Official Statistics*. | [10.2478/jos-2022-0039](https://doi.org/10.2478/jos-2022-0039) | T7 | CL-P3-03, CL-P3-07 | A |
| `savitsky2023` | Savitsky et al. (2023). Joint Point and Variance Estimation Under a Hierarchical Bayesian Model for Survey Count Data. *The Annals of Applied Statistics*. | [10.1214/22-AOAS1704](https://doi.org/10.1214/22-AOAS1704) | T7 | CL-P2-01 | B |
| `savitsky2024` | Savitsky et al. (2024). Bayesian Inference for Repeated Measures Under Informative Sampling. *Journal of Official Statistics*. | [10.1177/0282423X241235252](https://doi.org/10.1177/0282423X241235252) | T7 | CL-P3-03, CL-P3-07 | B |
| `savitsky2026` | Savitsky & Gershunskaya (2026). Simulation-based Calibration of Uncertainty Intervals under Approximate Bayesian Estimation. *Journal of Machine Learning Research*. | no DOI — Journal of Machine Learning Research article (v27, 2026); JMLR assigns no DOI to its articles (publisher policy), only a stable jmlr.org URL | T7 | CL-P3-02 | B |
| `schouten2018` | Schouten et al. (2018). A Bayesian Analysis of Design Parameters in Survey Data Collection. *Journal of Survey Statistics and Methodology*. | [10.1093/jssam/smy012](https://doi.org/10.1093/jssam/smy012) | T7 | CL-P3-03 | C |
| `shaby2014` | Shaby (2014). The open-faced sandwich adjustment for MCMC using estimating functions. *Journal of Computational and Graphical Statistics*. | [10.1080/10618600.2013.842174](https://doi.org/10.1080/10618600.2013.842174) | T7 | CL-P3-02, CL-P3-04, CL-P3-09 | A |
| `tamano2025` | Tamano & Tomo (2025). Location–scale calibration for generalized posterior. *arXiv*. | [10.48550/arXiv.2511.15320](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2511.15320) | T7 | CL-P3-02, CL-P3-04 | B |
| `tanaka2026` | Tanaka (2026). A theory of bootstrap coverage calibration for generalized posterior credible sets. *arXiv*. | [10.48550/arXiv.2606.25729](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2606.25729) | T8 | CL-P3-04, CL-P3-05, CL-P3-09 | A |
| `williams2020` | Williams & Savitsky (2020). Bayesian Estimation Under Informative Sampling with Unattenuated Dependence. *Bayesian Analysis*. | [10.1214/18-BA1143](https://doi.org/10.1214/18-BA1143) | T7 | CL-P3-03 | B |
| `williams2021` | Williams & Savitsky (2021). Uncertainty Estimation for Pseudo-Bayesian Inference Under Complex Sampling. *International Statistical Review*. | [10.1111/insr.12376](https://doi.org/10.1111/insr.12376) | T7 | CL-P3-02, CL-P3-03, CL-P3-06, CL-P3-07 | A |
| `williams2025b` | Williams et al. (2025). Uncertainty Quantification for Multi-level Models Using the Survey-Weighted Pseudo-Posterior. | [10.48550/arXiv.2510.09401](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2510.09401) | T7 | CL-P3-02, CL-P3-03, CL-P3-04, CL-P4-04 | B |
| `williams2025c` | Das et al. (2025). Scalable efficient inference in complex surveys through targeted resampling of weights. *arXiv*. | [10.48550/arXiv.2504.11636](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2504.11636) | T7 | CL-P3-03, CL-P3-04, CL-P3-05 | B |

## P4 — Why Bayesian RVE specifically  ·  23 papers {#sec-appendix-p4}

| Citekey | Reference | DOI | Theme | Claim-IDs served | Tier |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `acero2026` | Acero et al. (2026). Pseudo empirical best prediction of multiple characteristics in small areas. *arXiv*. | [10.48550/arXiv.2603.10136](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2603.10136) | T11 | CL-P4-01, CL-P4-03, CL-P4-04 | C |
| `das2024` | Das & Chambers (2024). Small Area Poverty Estimation Under Heteroskedasticity. *Journal of Survey Statistics and Methodology*. | [10.1093/jssam/smad045](https://doi.org/10.1093/jssam/smad045) | T9 | CL-P4-01, CL-P4-03, CL-P4-04 | C |
| `datta2025` | Datta et al. (2025). Small Area Estimation of Child Trafficking Proportions in Chiefdoms of Sierra Leone and Overall Ranking of the Chiefdoms. *Journal of Survey Statistics and Methodology*. | [10.1093/jssam/smaf013](https://doi.org/10.1093/jssam/smaf013) | T9 | CL-P4-01, CL-P4-04 | B |
| `fernandezcastilla2021` | Fernández-Castilla et al. (2021). Estimating outcome-specific effects in meta-analyses of multiple outcomes: A simulation study. *Behavior Research Methods*. | [10.3758/s13428-020-01459-4](https://doi.org/10.3758/s13428-020-01459-4) | T11 | CL-P1-04, CL-P1-05, CL-P2-05, CL-P4-03 | B |
| `gao2023` | Gao & Wakefield (2023). Pseudo-Bayesian unit level modeling for small area estimation under informative sampling. *arXiv*. | [10.48550/arXiv.2309.12119](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2309.12119) | T11 | CL-P3-03, CL-P4-01, CL-P4-03 | C |
| `giordano2024` | Giordano & Broderick (2024). The Bayesian Infinitesimal Jackknife for Variance. | [10.48550/arXiv.2305.06466](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2305.06466) | T7 | CL-P3-02, CL-P4-04 | A |
| `jacob2025` | Jacob et al. (2025). Quarterly Small Area Estimates of Extreme Poverty in Brazil Using Transformed Fay–Herriot Models. *Journal of Survey Statistics and Methodology*. | [10.1093/jssam/smaf025](https://doi.org/10.1093/jssam/smaf025) | T9 | CL-P4-01, CL-P4-03, CL-P4-04 | C |
| `ji2024` | Ji et al. (2025). Valid standard errors for Bayesian quantile regression with clustered and independent data. *Journal of Educational and Behavioral Statistics*. | [10.3102/10769986251379738](https://doi.org/10.3102/10769986251379738) | T7 | CL-P3-04, CL-P4-04 | B |
| `konstantopoulos2011` | Konstantopoulos (2011). Fixed effects and variance components estimation in three-level meta-analysis. *Research Synthesis Methods*. | [10.1002/jrsm.35](https://doi.org/10.1002/jrsm.35) | T11 | CL-P4-01, CL-P4-03 | B |
| `lee2026a` | Lee & Hooper (2026). Disentangling signal from noise: A Bayesian hybrid framework for variance decomposition in complex surveys with post-hoc domains. *Mathematics*. | [10.3390/math14030512](https://doi.org/10.3390/math14030512) | T11 | CL-P4-01, CL-P4-03, CL-P4-04 | C |
| `lee2026b` | Lee (2026). A Bayesian hierarchical hurdle beta-binomial model for survey-weighted bounded counts and its application to childcare enrollment. *arXiv*. | [10.48550/arXiv.2603.02611](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2603.02611) | T9 | CL-P3-06, CL-P3-07, CL-P4-04 | B |
| `lee2026c` | Lee (2026). Design effect ratios for Bayesian survey models: A diagnostic framework for identifying survey-sensitive parameters. *arXiv*. | [10.48550/arXiv.2603.07791](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2603.07791) | T9 | CL-P4-04, CL-P4-05 | A |
| `lewandowski2009` | Lewandowski et al. (2009). Generating Random Correlation Matrices Based on Vines and Extended Onion Method. *Journal of Multivariate Analysis*. | [10.1016/j.jmva.2009.04.008](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jmva.2009.04.008) | T10 | CL-P2-01 | B |
| `lopezlopez2018` | López‐López et al. (2018). Dealing with effect size multiplicity in systematic reviews and meta‐analyses. *Research Synthesis Methods*. | [10.1002/jrsm.1310](https://doi.org/10.1002/jrsm.1310) | T11 | CL-P2-05, CL-P4-03 | C |
| `luo2026` | Luo & Ji (2026). Robust standard errors for Bayesian posterior functionals via the infinitesimal jackknife. *arXiv*. | [10.48550/arXiv.2604.03398](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2604.03398) | T10 | CL-P3-04, CL-P3-09 | A |
| `mcnealis2026` | McNealis & Leger (2026). Smoothed Pseudo-Population Bootstrap Methods with Applications to Finite Population Quantiles. *Journal of Survey Statistics and Methodology*. | [10.1093/jssam/smaf036](https://doi.org/10.1093/jssam/smaf036) | T9 | CL-P4-01 | C |
| `raomolina2015` | Rao & Molina (2015). Small Area Estimation. *John Wiley & Sons*. | [10.1002/9781118735855](https://doi.org/10.1002/9781118735855) | T9 | CL-P4-01, CL-P4-03, CL-P4-04 | B |
| `sen2025` | Sen & Lahiri (2025). Estimation of Finite Population Proportions for Small Areas–-A Statistical Data Integration Approach. *Journal of Survey Statistics and Methodology*. | [10.1093/jssam/smae049](https://doi.org/10.1093/jssam/smae049) | T9 | CL-P4-01, CL-P4-03 | C |
| `song2020` | Song et al. (2020). Random-effects meta-analysis of combined outcomes based on reconstructions of individual patient data. *Research Synthesis Methods*. | [10.1002/jrsm.1406](https://doi.org/10.1002/jrsm.1406) | T11 | CL-P2-05, CL-P4-03 | C |
| `toth2025` | Toth et al. (2025). Bayesian Tree Models for Survey Sample Data. *Journal of Survey Statistics and Methodology*. | [10.1093/jssam/smae050](https://doi.org/10.1093/jssam/smae050) | T9 | CL-P4-01 | B |
| `vandennoortgate2015` | Van Den Noortgate et al. (2015). Meta-analysis of multiple outcomes: a multilevel approach. *Behavior Research Methods*. | [10.3758/s13428-014-0527-2](https://doi.org/10.3758/s13428-014-0527-2) | T11 | CL-P1-02, CL-P1-06, CL-P2-01, CL-P2-05, CL-P4-03 | B |
| `vanlissa2023` | Lissa et al. (2023). Selecting relevant moderators with Bayesian regularized meta-regression. *Research Synthesis Methods*. | [10.1002/jrsm.1628](https://doi.org/10.1002/jrsm.1628) | T11 | CL-P4-04 | A |
| `vergarahernandez2025` | Vergara-Hernandez et al. (2025). Taking Advantage of Sampling Designs in Spatial Small-Area Survey Studies. *Statistical Modelling*. | [10.1177/1471082X231226287](https://doi.org/10.1177/1471082X231226287) | T9 | CL-P4-01, CL-P4-03 | C |

## Pillar × theme (T1–T11) coverage matrix {#sec-appendix-matrix}

The pillar × theme coverage matrix. `n_owned` counts read-corpus papers in the cell; `coverage_status` flags cells that were **covered**, **thin**, or **none** during the literature assessment.

| Pillar | Theme | Theme label | n (owned) | Status | Claim-IDs |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| P1 | T1 | RVE foundations & small-sample | 10 | covered | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-02, CL-P1-03, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-05, CL-P1-06, CL-P1-07, CL-P4-03 |
| P1 | T2 | dependent effect sizes & RVE tutorials | 24 | covered | CL-P1-01, CL-P1-02, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-05, CL-P1-06, CL-P1-08, CL-P2-01, CL-P2-05 |
| P1 | T5 | Bayesian heterogeneity & covariance | 0 | none |  |
| P1 | T8 | sandwich / Godambe / estimating equations | 3 | covered | CL-P1-03, CL-P1-04, CL-P1-05, CL-P1-07, CL-P3-04, CL-P3-05 |
| P1 | T9 | small-area & multilevel Bayesian / DER diagnostics | 0 | none |  |
| P1 | T11 | meta-analysis with dependent outcomes | 0 | none |  |
| P2 | T3 | Bayesian hierarchical & priors | 4 | covered | CL-P2-01 |
| P2 | T4 | Bayesian network meta-analysis | 7 | covered | CL-P2-06, CL-P3-04, CL-P3-05 |
| P2 | T5 | Bayesian heterogeneity & covariance | 18 | covered | CL-P2-01, CL-P2-04, CL-P2-05 |
| P2 | T11 | meta-analysis with dependent outcomes | 1 | thin | CL-P2-05 |
| P3 | T4 | Bayesian network meta-analysis | 1 | thin | CL-P2-06 |
| P3 | T6 | calibrated-Bayes theory | 3 | covered | CL-P0-02, CL-P3-01, CL-P3-02 |
| P3 | T7 | survey-calibration / pseudo-Bayes | 18 | covered | CL-P3-03, CL-P3-04 |
| P3 | T8 | sandwich / Godambe / estimating equations | 1 | thin | CL-P3-04 |
| P4 | T7 | survey-calibration / pseudo-Bayes | 1 | thin | CL-P4-04 |
| P4 | T9 | small-area & multilevel Bayesian / DER diagnostics | 8 | covered | CL-P4-04 |
| P4 | T10 | Bayesian complex / dependent data | 1 | thin |  |
| P4 | T11 | meta-analysis with dependent outcomes | 5 | covered | CL-P2-05, CL-P4-03 |

## How this review was built (reading map) {#sec-appendix-readingmap}

The review was assembled through a disciplined, documented methodology:

1. **Framing.** The positioning thesis (the four pillars P1–P4), the 37-claim framework (`CL-Pn-NN`, the join hub), and the 27 overclaim guardrails (G-01…G-27) were established before discovery began, defining the evidentiary scope and discipline.
2. **Discovery.** The existing literature was indexed and the search extended across the four pillars, with the two gap claims adversarially audited; a literature database and a pillar × theme map were built (this appendix's ancestors).
3. **Corpus.** The reused corpus was staged, the acquired gap papers added, and the bibliography finalized to a **138-paper** registry.
4. **Reading.** All 138 papers were read into structured critique notes, each tagged to the claim-IDs it serves, with a coverage and caveat-conflict review.
5. **Synthesis.** The claim-to-evidence map, the four pillar briefs (P1–P4), the consolidated gap-and-contribution narrative, and the annotated outline were built and externally reviewed.
6. **Authoring.** The synthesis was written into this seven-chapter book, every load-bearing sentence anchored to a catalogued claim and an exact bibliographic citation, held to the guardrails and the locked gap wordings.

Every `@citekey` in the chapters resolves to a bibliography entry with a DOI (citation integrity verified); the two GAP-ASSERTIONs (`CL-P2-02`, `CL-P4-06`) rest on documented systematic absence across this catalogued corpus, not on any single row of the table above.
